# Exact Prompt Text Generated by MCP Server

This document shows the exact prompt text that the MCP server generates when you use the `use_datagrid_component` prompt.

## Example: Using with app.ts

If you call the prompt with:
- `componentPath`: "app.ts"
- `users`: Sample data array

The generated prompt text will be:

---

I want to use the datagrid component in the app component.

CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS:
- The DatagridComponent has selector: 'datagrid'
- In the parent component template, you MUST use: <datagrid [users]="users"></datagrid>
- Do NOT use <clr-datagrid> in the parent template - that's only used inside DatagridComponent's own template

User data to use:
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "creation": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z",
    "color": "Blue"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "name": "Jane Smith",
    "creation": "2024-02-20T00:00:00Z",
    "color": "Green"
  }
]

Current app.ts:
```typescript
import { Component, signal } from '@angular/core';
import { RouterOutlet } from '@angular/router';
import { ClarityModule } from '@clr/angular';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-root',
  imports: [RouterOutlet, ClarityModule],
  templateUrl: './app.html',
  styleUrl: './app.css'
})
export class App {
  protected readonly title = signal('angular-starter');
}
```

Current app.html:
```html
<div class="main-container">
  <header class="header header-6">
    <div class="branding">
      <a href="javascript:void(0)" class="nav-link">
        <span class="title">Angular Starter</span>
      </a>
    </div>
  </header>
  <div class="content-container">
    <div class="content-area">
      <div class="alert alert-info">
        <div class="alert-items">
          <div class="alert-item static">
            <div class="alert-icon-wrapper">
              <clr-icon shape="info-circle"></clr-icon>
            </div>
            <span class="alert-text">
              Clarity Design System installed successfully!
            </span>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <button class="btn btn-primary">Primary Button</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
<router-outlet></router-outlet>
```

Datagrid component definition:
```typescript
import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common';
import { Component, Input } from '@angular/core';
import { ClrDatagridModule, ClrIconModule } from '@clr/angular';

@Component({
    selector: 'datagrid',
    templateUrl: './datagrid.html',
    standalone: true,
    imports: [CommonModule, ClrDatagridModule, ClrIconModule],
})
export class DatagridComponent {
    @Input()users: any[] = [];
}
```

Note: The DatagridComponent's internal template uses <clr-datagrid>, but when using this component in a parent, you use <datagrid> (the component selector).

Required changes:
1. app.ts: 
   - Add: import { DatagridComponent } from './datagrid';
   - Add: users = [
  {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "creation": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z",
    "color": "Blue"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "name": "Jane Smith",
    "creation": "2024-02-20T00:00:00Z",
    "color": "Green"
  }
];
   - Add DatagridComponent to the @Component imports array

2. app.html:
   - Add exactly: <datagrid [users]="users"></datagrid>
   - Use 'datagrid' (the component selector), NOT 'clr-datagrid'

Example of correct usage in parent template:
```html
<datagrid [users]="users"></datagrid>
```

Component file path: D:\Documents\Angular\ts-mcp\angular\src\app\app.ts
Template file path: D:\Documents\Angular\ts-mcp\angular\src\app\app.html

---

## Key Points in the Prompt

1. **CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS** section at the top emphasizes:
   - Use `<datagrid [users]="users"></datagrid>` 
   - Do NOT use `<clr-datagrid>`

2. **User data** is provided as JSON

3. **Current component files** are shown for context

4. **DatagridComponent definition** is shown to clarify the component structure

5. **Note** explains the difference between `<clr-datagrid>` (internal) and `<datagrid>` (parent usage)

6. **Required changes** section lists exact steps

7. **Example** shows the correct HTML syntax

8. **File paths** are included for reference

This prompt is designed to be very explicit about using `<datagrid>` instead of `<clr-datagrid>` in parent components.
